text.title: rust-tokio-async Core Rules
text.summary: 9 testable rules grounding the rust-tokio-async methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Classify the workload (fixed-small / CPU-heavy / large-bounded / dynamic) before writing code. Pick the matching primitive.
rule.rationale: Wrong primitive either over-engineers small N or OOM-risks large N.

rule.statement: Use tokio::try_join! for fixed N <= 20 futures collected together, short-circuiting on the first error.
rule.rationale: try_join! resolves all futures simultaneously; large N overloads pools.

rule.statement: Wrap CPU-heavy work (Argon2, bcrypt, regex compile, image, zip) in tokio::task::spawn_blocking; await with .await?? to propagate both join error and inner Result.
rule.rationale: CPU futures starve the executor thread, blocking other tasks.

rule.statement: Process large collections with futures::stream::buffer_unordered(N) + tokio::sync::Semaphore::acquire_owned for explicit concurrency control.
rule.rationale: Semaphore adds an explicit capacity token visible to logging/metrics.

rule.statement: Use tokio::task::JoinSet instead of Vec<JoinHandle<T>>. JoinSet cancels remaining tasks on drop, preventing leaks.
rule.rationale: Vec<JoinHandle> requires explicit abort() loops; forgetting them leaves tasks running.

rule.statement: Wrap every external I/O call in tokio::time::timeout — no safe default for a missing timeout.
rule.rationale: A hung external call exhausts Tokio worker threads.

rule.statement: In tokio::select! with non-cancellation-safe futures, document each branch's cancel-safety with a comment.
rule.rationale: select! drops the losing future at the next .await, silently losing partial state.

rule.statement: Never hold std::sync::MutexGuard across .await. Prefer mpsc, or use tokio::sync::Mutex when async-aware locks are required.
rule.rationale: std::sync::MutexGuard is !Send; holding it across .await compile-errors or deadlocks.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
